svg.style: background: #ffffff; background-color: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); color-scheme: light dark;

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 15px; margin-left: 401px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #111827;
display: inline-block; font-size: 20px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#111827, #d4dae7); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: nowrap;

div.div: The loops stack — and how the Workshop Agent design used them

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 608px; height: 1px; padding-top: 56px; margin-left: 102px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #6b7280;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#6b7280, #868c98); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font: A meta-tool isn't one loop. It's several, nested: each loop wraps the one inside it, and the outermost (hill-climbing) reaches in to improve them.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 251, 235), rgb(25, 22, 8)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(245, 158, 11), rgb(162, 87, 0));
fill: light-dark(rgb(240, 253, 250), rgb(14, 25, 23)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(20, 184, 166), rgb(0, 141, 126));
fill: light-dark(rgb(250, 245, 255), rgb(27, 23, 31)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(168, 85, 247), rgb(185, 114, 253));
fill: light-dark(rgb(239, 246, 255), rgb(21, 27, 35)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(59, 130, 246), rgb(76, 137, 236));

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(180, 83, 9), rgb(223, 139, 76)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(180, 83, 9), rgb(223, 139, 76));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 168px; height: 1px; padding-top: 309px; margin-left: 713px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #b45309;
display: inline-block; font-size: 10px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#b45309, #df8b4c); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-style: italic; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

font.b:
Loop 4 reaches in
and rewrites the
inner loops

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 706px; height: 1px; padding-top: 127px; margin-left: 25px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font:
④ ↺ Hill-climbing loop — past runs rewrite the harness, so it improves itself
▸ workshop: each run sharpens the capture-and-rebuild skill for next time

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 674px; height: 1px; padding-top: 191px; margin-left: 79px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font:
③ ↺ Event-driven loop — a trigger starts it: a schedule, a webhook, a new file
▸ workshop: the next workshop captures itself from a calendar invite

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 621px; height: 1px; padding-top: 263px; margin-left: 132px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font:
② ↺ Verification loop — a separate checker grades the output and sends it back
▸ workshop: a scored test checks me; a second pass checks the course

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 400px; height: 1px; padding-top: 380px; margin-left: 202px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.font:
① ↺ Agent loop
reason → act → observe → repeat, until the goal is met
▸ workshop: transcribe, snapshot, then build the tutor · notebook · test
